channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 3 World Cup rivals find 'Common Ground' in a cross-border beer
item.description: A trio of craft breweries from the global soccer event's three host nations are using the tournament to brew something increasingly rare: cross-border solidarity.
item.content:encoded:
A trio of craft breweries from the global soccer event's three host nations are using the tournament to brew something increasingly rare: cross-border solidarity.

(Image credit: Justin Gellerson for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Could neo-Nazi youth, or 'active clubs,' have played a role in Belfast riots?
item.description: In the wake of racist, mob violence in Northern Ireland this month, there has been a fresh round of attention on so-called "active clubs," the global network of fascist, white nationalist youth groups that center their activities around mixed martial arts.
item.content:encoded:
In the wake of racist, mob violence in Northern Ireland this month, there has been a fresh round of attention on so-called "active clubs," the global network of fascist, white nationalist youth groups that center their activities around mixed martial arts.

(Image credit: Charles McQuillan)
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: People are taking allergy and heartburn pills for PMS. Could it work?
item.description: Can the mix really help with premenstrual woes? Theoretically, the link between histamine and hormones could explain why the trendy DIY treatment seems to help some women.
item.content:encoded: Can the mix really help with premenstrual woes? Theoretically, the link between histamine and hormones could explain why the trendy DIY treatment seems to help some women.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden

item.title: Why do some U.S. airports have private security, but others use TSA?
item.description: About 20 U.S. airports rely on private security companies rather than the Transportation Security Administration to ensure that passengers and cargo comply with federal aviation safety standards.
item.content:encoded:
About 20 U.S. airports rely on private security companies rather than the Transportation Security Administration to ensure that passengers and cargo comply with federal aviation safety standards.

(Image credit: Arin Yoon for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Red, white and glowing blue: Trump's push for new reactors reaches the finish line
item.description: A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.
item.content:encoded:
A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.

(Image credit: Valar Atomics)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes over the weekend, the search for survivors continues after the devastating earthquakes in Venezuela, Trump's immigration agenda gets boost from SCOTUS.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes over the weekend, the search for survivors continues after the devastating earthquakes in Venezuela, Trump's immigration agenda gets boost from SCOTUS.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Israel moves to formally recognize Armenian WWI deaths as a genocide
item.description: Israel's Cabinet approved a proposal to designate violence against Armenians by the Ottoman Empire during World War I as a genocide. The step reflects deteriorating ties between Israel and Turkey.
item.content:encoded:
Israel's Cabinet approved a proposal to designate violence against Armenians by the Ottoman Empire during World War I as a genocide. The step reflects deteriorating ties between Israel and Turkey.

(Image credit: Oded Balilty)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Australia to double potential fines over child social media accounts
item.description: Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts.
item.content:encoded:
Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts.

(Image credit: Rick Rycroft)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A 'heat dome' is driving dangerous heat across the U.S. into the July 4 weekend
item.description: Dangerous heat is expected across large swaths of the U.S. this week, according to the National Weather Service.
item.content:encoded:
Dangerous heat is expected across large swaths of the U.S. this week, according to the National Weather Service.

(Image credit: Adam Gray)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: In Venezuela, silence has become a rescue tool
item.description: In Venezuela rescue crews now stop almost everything and ask for silence so they can hear anyone still alive underneath the rubble. NPR's Eyder Peralta reports from the port city of La Guaira.
item.content:encoded: In Venezuela rescue crews now stop almost everything and ask for silence so they can hear anyone still alive underneath the rubble. NPR's Eyder Peralta reports from the port city of La Guaira.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta
